data.title: Deutsche Bank CEO Christian Sewing to face multimillion-pound lawsuit - report
UK's retail sales rise 0.7% Y/Y in August
data.description:
• Retail sales in the UK increased 0.70% in August of 2025 over the same month in the previous year, more than estimates of 0.6%.
  • M/M retail sales increased 0.50% in August of 2025.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Coca-Cola bottler to reportedly cut 600+ jobs in South Africa
data.description:
• A bottler for Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) plans to cut more than 600 jobs in South Africa, Business Day reported, citing Food and Allied Workers Union spokesperson Dominique Swartz.
  • Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa has sent workers a notice of possible retrenchment and started a consultation process, the newspaper reported. A final decision hasn’t been made, it said.
  • CCBSA has reportedly cited financial pressures as behind its decision to also shut down plants in Bloemfontein and East London as part of a broader restructuring effort.

data.title: SoftBank Vision Fund to lay off 20% of workforce in shift to bold AI bets - report
data.description:
• SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) to lay off nearly 20% of its Vision Fund team globally as it moves resources to founder Masayoshi Son’s large-scale artificial intelligence bets in the United States, according to a memo seen by Reuters and a source familiar with the plan.
  • The cuts mark the third round of layoffs at the Japanese investment conglomerate’s flagship fund since 2022.
  • While the fund will continue to make new bets, remaining staff will dedicate more resources to Son’s ambitious AI initiatives, such as the proposed $500 billion Stargate project - an initiative to build a vast network of U.S. data centers in partnership with OpenAI (MSFT), the source added.
  • The development comes as SoftBank's (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) joint venture with OpenAI in Japan, which aims to bring AI services to corporate customers, is running significantly behind schedule, according to a separate Reuters report.

data.title: CHARBONE Hydrogen announces $2M convertible debentures replacement
data.description:
• CHARBONE HYDROGEN (TSXV:CH:CA) announced on Thursday the signature of replacement debentures of an amount of $2.05M by amending certain terms of the secured convertible debentures of the company that the company issued in connection with the private placement of debentures of an aggregate principal amount of $1.75M of 12% secured convertible debentures.
  •  Under the new Replacement Debenture:
  • The maturity date has been extended from September 30 and October 31, 2025 to September 30, 2026; 
  • The convertible balance moves from $1.7 million to $2.1 million with the same annual rate of 12%, payable monthly, and 
  • The conversion price of the debentures moves from $0.10 per debenture share to $0.07 per debenture share.

data.title: Asian indexes mostly lower after Intel helped Wall Street to new records
data.description:
Asia indexes traded mostly lower on Friday after a rally of technology stocks led by Nvidia (NVDA) and Intel (INTC) pushed Wall Street to more records.

Gold edged up to around $3,650 per ounce on Friday but held most of its two-day decline, positioning the metal on track to pause a four-week rally. 

Japan's Nikkei (NKY:IND) fell 0.67%, with economic numbers coming in. Japan's inflation eases to 2.7% in August. The BOJ left interest rate steady at 0.5% as expected.

The Japanese yen strengthened past 147.5 per dollar on Friday. Japan 10 Year Government Bond Yield increased to 1.64%, the highest since July 2008.

China (SHCOMP) gained 0.10%, with mainland shares lacking clear direction as investors searched for fresh catalysts. China has reportedly urged its top hog producers to play a leading role in reducing output as the country grapples with excess supply and weak demand in its massive pork sector.

China’s 10-year government bond yield fell to around 1.85% on Friday. The offshore yuan depreciated past 7.11 per dollar, extending its decline from the previous session amid a stronger greenback.

Hong Kong (HSI) gained 0.12%, rebounding from a 1.4% slump in the previous session as bargain hunters stepped in. 

Exports from Malaysia grew by 1.9% yoy to MYR 131.6 billion in August.

The Bank of Korea plans to broaden its forward guidance by introducing a dot plot of future interest rate paths, Governor Rhee Chang-yong said Thursday.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.41%. The U.S. may soon remove its 25% penal tariff on Indian goods and cut reciprocal duties to 10–15% from 25%, India’s Chief Economic Adviser V. Anantha Nageswaran said Thursday.

The United Arab Emirates' economy is now expected to grow 4.9% in 2025.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.70%, recouping some losses from the prior session as dovish bets on domestic monetary policy supported equities. The Australian dollar slipped to around $0.660.

New Zealand’s finance minister is set to name a new central bank chief in the coming weeks. New Zealand’s trade deficit shrank to NZD 1.2 billion in August.

In the U.S. on Thursday, Wall Street's three major averages closed at record highs, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points. Stocks were propelled by gains on big tech, and cybersecurity.

U.S. stock futures gained on Friday, supported by the Federal Reserve’s latest rate cut: Dow +0.12%; S&P 500 +0.07%; Nasdaq +0.07%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust announces offering of $200M of series AP debentures
data.description:
• RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX:REI.UN:CA) said on Thursday that it has agreed to issue $200M principal amount of Series AP senior unsecured debentures. 
  • The debentures will be sold at a price of $100 per $100 principal amount, carry a coupon of 4.417% per annum, and will mature on October 1, 2032.
  • The net proceeds of the debentures being offered will be used by the trust to repay existing indebtedness at or prior to maturity. The balance of the net proceeds, if any, will be used for general business purposes.

data.title: Lithium Chile announces upsized life offering of up to $6.5M
data.description:
• Lithium Chile (TSXV:LITH:CA) said on Thursday that it has agreed with its agents, Canaccord Genuity and Moneta Securities, to increase the size of the previously announced "best efforts" private placement. 
  • As a result of this amendment, the offering will consist of up to 13M units of the company at a price of $0.50 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $6.5M. 
  • The company has also granted the agents an option, exercisable in whole or in part at any time prior to the closing of the offering, to arrange for the sale of up to an additional 1.95M units at the offering price, for additional gross proceeds of up to $975,000.
  • The net proceeds of the offering will be used to advance the company's existing mineral properties in Argentina and Chile, for general and administrative expenses, and for working capital purposes.

data.title: NGEx receives court approval for the spin-out of royalties
data.description:
• NGEx Minerals (TSX:NGEX:CA) said on Thursday that it has obtained a final order from the Supreme Court of British Columbia approving the previously announced spin-out transaction pursuant to which the company will spin-out net smelter returns royalties on the Lunahuasi and Los Helados projects into a wholly-owned subsidiary of NGEx, LunR Royalties. 
  • It is anticipated that NGEx shareholders holding NGEx shares as of the business day immediately prior to the effective date of the arrangement will be entitled to receive the New NGEx shares and the LunR Royalties shares pursuant to the arrangement.

data.title: BOJ leaves interest rate steady at 0.5% as expected
data.description:
The Bank of Japan left its benchmark short-term interest rate steady at 0.5% during the September 2025 meeting, maintaining borrowing costs at the highest level recorded since 2008 and in line with market expectations.

The policy rate remaining at 0.5% signals the continuation of the Bank of Japan's cautious approach to tightening after years of ultra-low and negative interest rates. 

 At Friday's meeting, the BoJ announced it would begin selling its holdings of exchange-traded funds ((ETFs)) and real-estate investment trusts ((REITs)).

The board noted Japan’s economy had recovered moderately despite some weakness. Private consumption was resilient, helped by improving employment and income. Exports and industrial output remained subdued, with front-loading ahead of U.S. tariff hikes and a subsequent pullback.

Separately, Japan's annual inflation rate eased to 2.7% in August 2025 from 3.1% in the previous month, marking the lowest reading since October 2024.
